title: Color of LCL in Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Color of LCL in Rebuild             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Aug 14, 2012 5:03 pm So everyone's been assuming that the color of LCL was changed from orange to red in Rebuild, due to the red color of the ocean and a few other factors. But has this color change been officially confirmed? I'm having some doubts about this due to one major thing I noticed. While looking through 1.0 in order to answer a question in another thread, I happened upon the scene in which Misato reveals the existence of Lilith to Shinji. In this scene, the liquid spilling out of Lilith's truncated abdomen was not red, but orange . This could easily be explained away by lighting or a simple animation error, but it seems a bit too conspicuous to me. The liquid which filled Eva-01's entry plug - identified as LCL - was indeed tinted red, so could we conclude that these are two different substances? Any input would be appreciated.

[]:             Quote Post by Joe » Tue Aug 14, 2012 6:00 pm Maybe that the LCL changes color over time. Fresh LCL (spilling out of Lilith) is orange, and "old" LCL is red. Also, the entry-plug's LCL is ionised , it could affect the color.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Aug 14, 2012 6:09 pm Joe wrote: Maybe that the LCL changes color over time. Fresh LCL (spilling out of Lilith) is orange, and "old" LCL is red. Also, the entry-plug's LCL is ionised , it could affect the color.

[]:             Quote Post by Joe » Tue Aug 14, 2012 6:18 pm Effectively. my assumptions are false so. ... I think the color change of the LCL transcends our understanding.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Aug 14, 2012 6:24 pm Joe wrote: I think the color change of the LCL transcends our understanding.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 14, 2012 7:06 pm It was mentioned a couple of other places in passing but it's probably made to evoke a connection to "blood". The Angels now all explode in a mass of blood. It's probably something they couldn't get away with during NGE's TV run since TV Tokyo wasn't took keen on there being any kind of blood in the show (Cowboy Bebop was originally cut short because TV Tokyo was squeamish about the violence).

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Tue Aug 28, 2012 5:50 pm I was always a fan of the 'rebuild is a continuation of the series' theory. As a result I thought the ocean being red had to do with EoE and Rei/Lilith falling apart etc etc. Whether it be an ocean of LCL, blood, or a mix. I read somewhere the second impact was said to kill off all life in the ocean (in rebuild anyway, can't remember where I read it ><) So that might have something to do with it. Just my 2 cents but I'd love to read more opinions on it :p p.s. New here so I havn't had much time to look around through old threads. Yea, just did some more research so I'm completely for red ocean=caused by second impact.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Aug 28, 2012 6:41 pm Ornette wrote: It was mentioned a couple of other places in passing but it's probably made to evoke a connection to "blood". The Angels now all explode in a mass of blood. It's probably something they couldn't get away with during NGE's TV run since TV Tokyo wasn't took keen on there being any kind of blood in the show (Cowboy Bebop was originally cut short because TV Tokyo was squeamish about the violence).

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Aug 30, 2012 3:02 pm maybe it's just like water is mostly colorless when it's only a little, but blue when there's a lot of it. Most likely a) they just forgot about to change the color of the "waterfall" flowing directly out of lillith or b) it's still the same orange-ish red, but how red or how orange it looks in an individual scene depends on the lighting. The shiny graphix of Rebuild allow them to do far more with the lighting. (I never really noticed how *dark* Rei's appartment and the entry plugs have to be...) and the inside of the plugs is really dark, so the lcl in it would look really red. Also, the stuff in the ocean and the angel explosions is Adam-based. Maybe Adam's primal liquid stuff is just red while Lillith's is orange?

[]:             Quote Post by Son of Lilin » Fri Aug 31, 2012 5:43 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Since LCL is supposed to be composed of the basic building blocks of life, I don't see how it could deteriorate over time.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Aug 31, 2012 6:27 pm Son of Lilin wrote: where did you even hear this

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun Sep 02, 2012 12:22 am I'm sorry the liquid effects largely disappears. With the increased budget, you could show it interacting with hair, or Shinji's street clothes. Could be a cool visual.

[]:             Quote Post by Pandymint » Sun Sep 09, 2012 3:20 am Correct me if I am wrong, but I think I remember reading somewhere that fans of the series associated the LCL with the powder drink Tang due to its orange color. Then, to counteract this, he had the color of LCL changed to red to invoke a visual association with blood and break it away from being called 'Tang.' Lurkis wrote: I was always a fan of the 'rebuild is a continuation of the series' theory. As a result I thought the ocean being red had to do with EoE and Rei/Lilith falling apart etc etc. Whether it be an ocean of LCL, blood, or a mix.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Sep 09, 2012 8:56 am All this talk of LCL color takes me back to the days before 1.0's release when there was a very heavy belief on this forum that the LCL in Rebuild was colored Black.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 09, 2012 7:04 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: All this talk of LCL color takes me back to the days before 1.0's release when there was a very heavy belief on this forum that the LCL in Rebuild was colored Black.
